bundles.title: How To Create a Screenplay
bundles.summary: Callable research background bundle for broad screenplay-creation questions, theory-support requests, and agent-skill design work.
when_to_load: User asks broad questions such as 'how to create a screenplay' and wants theory support rather than one immediate screenplay artifact.
User is designing or auditing the screenplay Agent Skill and needs a bounded background bundle before choosing narrower routes.
A survey_pack would otherwise expand blindly without a single declared research anchor.
stop_rules: Do not auto-load this bundle for narrow drafting, polishing, or rewrite requests that already have a stable primary route.
Do not expand beyond this bundle unless the next asset changes the next decision materially.
If the user's real need becomes a concrete artifact, exit the bundle and route to the narrower workflow.

bundles.title: Chinese Genre Foundations
bundles.summary: Background bundle for Chinese genre screenwriting: suspense (悬疑), wuxia (武侠), thriller (惊悚), and their craft intersections.
when_to_load: User requests Chinese genre screenwriting (悬疑, 武侠, 惊悚) and needs genre-specific craft guidance.
User asks about differences between Chinese genre conventions and Western genre conventions.
A genre adaptation or cross-cultural rewrite requires Chinese genre knowledge.
stop_rules: Do not load for non-Chinese genre requests.
Do not expand beyond the three genre atoms unless the user explicitly requests broader genre survey.
If the user's real need is a concrete artifact, exit the bundle and route to the narrower workflow.

bundles.title: Shortform Platform Production
bundles.summary: Production background for shortform video scripts: platform-specific constraints (Douyin, Reels/TikTok), hook strategies, and format adaptation.
when_to_load: User requests shortform video scripts for specific platforms (抖音, Reels, TikTok).
A commercial script needs platform-specific hook and format optimization.
User is adapting longform content to shortform platform format.
stop_rules: Do not load for non-shortform requests.
Do not load all platform atoms if only one platform is targeted.
Stop after platform-specific atoms; do not expand to full commercial workflow unless needed.

bundles.title: Cross-Medium Adaptation
bundles.summary: Adaptation strategy bundle: cross-medium transformation (film↔game↔interactive↔documentary), engine preservation, and genre adaptation techniques.
when_to_load: User requests adaptation from one medium to another (film→game, game→interactive, etc.).
User asks how to preserve core story engine across medium boundaries.
A documentary or interactive adaptation requires specific medium constraint knowledge.
stop_rules: Do not load for same-medium rewrites or polish requests.
Do not expand to full genre survey unless the user requests multi-genre comparison.
If adaptation direction is unclear, ask clarifying question before loading.

bundles.title: Systematic Rewrite Toolkit
bundles.summary: Rewrite and polish toolkit: diagnosis workflows, tone-to-writing-moves mapping, rubrics for rewrite/quality gate, and incremental improvement strategies.
when_to_load: User requests rewrite, polish, or diagnosis of existing script pages.
User says dialogue feels flat, pacing is off, or tone is inconsistent.
A quality gate check or systematic rewrite requires structured diagnosis tools.
stop_rules: Do not load for first-draft generation requests.
Do not expand to full craft survey; limit to tone + pacing + exposition + dialogue subtext.
If rewrite scope is unclear, narrow to one dimension first.

bundles.title: Team Collaboration & Multi-Agent Workflows
bundles.summary: Multi-agent and team collaboration workflows: expert subagent casting, team workflow design, story memory checkpoints for cross-agent handoff.
when_to_load: User asks about multi-agent screenwriting, writer's room simulation, or team collaboration design.
A project requires checkpoint-based continuity across multiple agents or sessions.
User wants to design expert subagent dispatch for complex screenplay projects.
stop_rules: Do not load for single-artifact, single-agent generation requests.
Do not expand to full team topology unless the user explicitly requests team design.
If the user's real need is a single artifact, use the narrowest workflow.
